subject: lonely retirees
one_liner: Swipe-based companionship for people who have outlived their patience for small talk.
brand_voice_notes: Clinical, unhurried, faintly actuarial. Reads like a Medicare supplement brochure that discovered romance. Never winks. The product is sincere about being slow.

hero.sub: Linger pairs you with other people who are also still here. One match a day, delivered before dinner.

features.title: Slow Mode
features.blurb: Modern apps overwhelm. Linger shows you exactly one person per day, at 5:15 AM, whether you are awake or not. If neither of you responds for 30 days, we log it as a connection.

features.title: Grandchild Oversight
features.blurb: Designate up to three grandchildren as account administrators. Matches unlock after a majority vote. Quorum rules apply. Abstentions count as no.

features.title: Acquaintance Cross-Reference
features.blurb: Every match is checked against everyone you have ever known. 60% of our users match with someone they already met in 1974. We consider this efficient.

pricing_tiers.name: Early Bird
perks: Full access during the matching window
One complimentary conversation starter per week ("How was traffic?")

pricing_tiers.name: Gold Years
perks: Matches every day, including federal holidays
Priority placement in your former coworkers' card stacks

pricing_tiers.name: Perpetuity
pricing_tiers.tagline: Billed one time. Lasts the rest of your life.
perks: Term length determined actuarially at signup

testimonials.quote: I matched with Harold. He lives in my building. We had already met.
testimonials.name: Carol Anne Petty
testimonials.title: Retired Regional Claims Adjudicator, Boca Raton

testimonials.quote: The grandchild approval feature rejected four perfectly nice women. My grandson is twelve.
testimonials.name: Eugene Vance
testimonials.title: Former Assistant Harbor Master (Inland)

testimonials.quote: Slow Mode showed me one man per day for a year. I married my mail carrier instead. Five stars.
testimonials.name: Dorothy Klepp
testimonials.title: Church Phone Tree Coordinator, Emerita

demo_screen_concept: A one-card-per-day swipe screen: today's single match as a large profile card (deadpan senior portrait, name, age, 'preferred dinner time' and shared-acquaintances-since-1968 count, a 'compatibility' meter measured in canasta terms), with a Grandchild Oversight side panel showing each designated grandchild's vote status (Approved / Pending / Abstained), and a countdown to tomorrow's 5:15 AM match drop. Swiping is rate-limited to one decision per day; after you decide, the card is replaced by a 'That is enough for today' rest screen.

images.rationale: hero skipped: vintage-editorial masthead is typographic and carries itself; demo is photo-shaped (one daily match card) so 1 portrait for today's match; remaining budget on 3 testimonial headshots, one per named testimonial. 4/4 budget used, all passed vision judge on first attempt.

items.prompt: Editorial photograph, deadpan vintage portrait of a kind elderly man in his late 70s seated slightly too formally in a mustard cardigan and pressed collared shirt, hands folded in his lap, faint hopeful smile, plain aged-cream studio backdrop in warm tone hex F7F0E3, warm muted brick and coffee-brown palette hex 8C3B2E and 3E2F23, soft window light, 1970s community-newsletter portrait mood, one focal subject. No text, no letters, no numbers, no logos, no watermarks, no rendered UI screens.

No text, no letters, no numbers, no logos, no watermarks, no rendered UI screens.

No text, no letters, no numbers, no logos, no watermarks, no rendered UI screens.

design_direction.style: vintage-editorial brochure (retirement-community print aesthetic, magazine masthead hero, numbered sections, hairline rules)
design_direction.archetype: editorial magazine landing — masthead hero with oversized serif headline, brochure-style numbered features, pull-quote testimonials, table-like pricing
design_direction.motion_vocab: unhurried: 350-400ms ease-out fades, gentle underline reveals on hover, no parallax, no bounce; respects prefers-reduced-motion
design_direction.hero_composition_notes: Centered masthead like a community newsletter front page: small all-caps kicker ('EST. THIS YEAR'), enormous Abril Fatface h1, hairline rules above and below, optional sepia portrait to the side. Anti-sameness check: no prior entries exist in this site, tuple trivially unique.
